commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Prop name mismatch: 'ui.tsx' passes 'temp' to 'CurrentWeather', but the component destructures 'temperature'. This results in the main temperature value being undefined/NaN in the UI.
issues.fix: Align prop names: either use 'temp' or 'temperature' consistently across the component and its caller.

issues.description: Component Redundancy: 'CurrentWeather.tsx' contains a full implementation of the card (including humidity, wind, and forecast). 'ui.tsx' then renders this component AND manually adds another 'WeatherDetailItem' and 'ForecastStrip' below it, resulting in a UI that displays all data twice.
issues.fix: Refactor 'CurrentWeather' to only render the hero section (city/temp/icon) and let the parent 'WeatherCard' manage the layout of sub-components.

issues.description: Type inconsistencies: The 'hooks.ts' and 'entrypoint.tsx' files refer to a 'Props' type which is not exported/defined in 'types.d.ts' (only 'WeatherCardProps' exists).
issues.fix: Export 'type Props = WeatherCardProps' in types.d.ts or use the full name consistently.

issues.description: Missing hover/active states on the card or forecast items to indicate they are interactive or to provide visual feedback.
issues.fix: Add a 'hover' background change to the Card or forecast item stacks using design tokens.

evaluations.critique: The component follows the design system tokens well but suffers from significant architectural issues. There is massive redundancy where sub-components reimplement the entire card's logic, and a naming mismatch in props ('temp' vs 'temperature') causes the primary data point to fail to render.
